title: Smoky Tender Russian Pork Shoulder Roast
description: A festive Russian-inspired pork shoulder roast with a smoky dry rub, slow-cooked to tender perfection for a family gathering.
instructions: Combine salt, pepper, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, brown sugar, thyme, and bay leaves in a bowl.
Rub the pork shoulder thoroughly with the dry spice mix, pressing it into the meat and letting it sit for 30 minutes at room temperature.
Heat a skillet over medium-high and sear the pork on all sides until a brown crust forms, about 5 minutes per side.
Place the seared pork in a roasting pan, add water and vinegar to the bottom, and cover tightly with foil.
Smoke the pork in a preheated oven at 120 °C (250 °F) for 3 hours, basting every 45 minutes with pan juices.
After 3 hours, remove the foil, increase the oven temperature to 190 °C (375 °F) and roast for an additional 30 minutes until the surface is caramelized.
Use a meat thermometer to confirm the internal temperature reaches 85 °C (185 °F) for maximum tenderness.
Rest the roast for 15 minutes before carving, then serve with roasted root vegetables and a side of sauerkraut.
